text: The team has fixed the bug.
We have deployed a fix this morning.
Hi Patrícia, thanks for the quick call. Can you give me an update on the login ticket?
Of course. Good news, we have resolved the issue. The dashboard is working again.
That is a relief. What was the root cause?
A recent update changed one security setting. We have reproduced the bug and checked the logs to be sure.
And it will not happen again?
We have applied a fix and tested it twice. I have also added a note to the ticket for the team.

Hi Patrícia, this is David from the Atlas team. I am leaving a quick message before our call. We have a problem this morning. Several people on the sales team cannot log in to the dashboard, and they all see the same error. It started around nine, and it has stopped their work for almost two hours now, so I have marked the ticket as urgent. I have not changed anything on our side, so I think the issue is on the platform. Could you tell me if your engineers have reproduced the bug, and if there is a workaround we can use right now? My team is waiting, and I would like an update as soon as you have one. Thanks, and talk soon.

Hi Patrícia, it is Sofia from technical support, with the update you asked for. Good news first: we have resolved the login issue. Here is the short story. We have reproduced the bug on a test account, so we could see the exact error. Then we have checked the server logs, and they have shown the real root cause. A recent update changed one security setting by mistake, and that blocked the login. While we looked for a permanent fix, we have shared a quick workaround, so most users could log in again within the hour. This afternoon we have applied the fix and tested it twice. Everything is stable now. I have added a full note to the ticket, so please tell David the problem has not come back. Let us follow up tomorrow to confirm.
So far, we have identified the root cause.
The team has resolved the issue.
We have reproduced the bug on a test account.
We applied a fix this morning.
Have you checked the logs yet?
We have found the root cause.
I have escalated this to our engineers.
We have shared a quick workaround.
Good news, we have resolved the issue.
